title: Siddhi Fork In The Road
definition: The divergence in spiritual practice between seeking psychic or magical abilities and achieving freedom from the self.

difficulty_levels.beginner: There is a choice in spiritual practice: either to seek powers like mind-reading or control over the elements, or to focus on freedom from the self. The Buddha chose the latter.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The Siddhi Fork in the Road refers to the decision faced by spiritual practitioners between developing psychic abilities (siddhis) and seeking freedom from the self.
difficulty_levels.expert: The Siddhi Fork in the Road is a philosophical juncture where spiritual practice can lead either towards the development of supernatural powers or the pursuit of self-liberation.

extra_fields.insight: Buddhist teachings differentiate between seeking magical abilities and pursuing freedom from the self, with the latter being considered more valuable.
